cues.narration: Welcome to ArtsFolio Training Video 05: Messages, Contact Submissions, and Email Signups. In this video, we’ll review visitor messages, test the public contact form, manage mailing-list signups, and protect private information.
Click Messages in the sidebar. This page collects submissions from your public Contact page.
Review sender information, the submitted message, and the date received directly in the list. There is no separate message-detail page.
Treat the message list as private workspace because submissions may contain names, email addresses, phone numbers, and personal details.
Use Clear carefully when removing old or test submissions. Confirm that the records are no longer needed before clearing the list.
Export CSV creates a downloadable copy of the message list. Store that file securely and delete it when it is no longer needed.
Open the public Contact page and review it as a visitor would.
Check that the form fields are clearly labeled, easy to complete, and appropriate for the kinds of inquiries you want to receive.
Contact instructions should explain what visitors can ask about, such as exhibitions, commissions, purchases, press, or studio visits.
Do not place private phone numbers, home addresses, or personal schedules on the public page unless you deliberately want them published.
When testing the form, use clearly marked test information. After submission, confirm that the visitor receives a sensible success message and that the message appears in the admin area.
Return to Messages and confirm that the test submission arrived. Delete or clearly label test records so they are not mistaken for real inquiries.
Click Email Signups to review people who joined your mailing list.
The signup list may include an email address, name, source, status, and signup date. Use this information only for the purpose the visitor agreed to.
Respect inactive, unsubscribed, or suppressed records. Do not manually reactivate someone who has asked not to receive email.
Source information can help you understand where people joined, but it should not become a surveillance scrapbook. Collect only what is useful.
When export or management tools are available, handle the resulting file carefully. Mailing lists are private records, not studio confetti.
Review the mailing-list prompt on the public site. It should explain what the visitor is joining without exaggeration or mystery.
Check that the signup form is readable, works on a phone, and does not request more information than you need.
The mailing-list prompt can be suppressed from Settings when you are not actively collecting signups.
A useful routine is to review new messages regularly, respond outside ArtsFolio when appropriate, update their status, and keep test submissions separate from real inquiries.
For email signups, honor consent, protect exported files, and remove access when a helper no longer needs it.
Finally, inspect the Contact page and signup controls at phone size. Labels should remain readable, fields should fit the screen, and buttons should remain easy to tap.
You now know how to review contact messages, test the public form, manage email signups, and protect visitor information. Next, we’ll cover sales, orders, analytics, and refunds.
